If provided for tenants using the New Universal Login experience, the email template and UI displays application details, and the user is prompted to redirect to the application's default login route after the ticket is used. client_id is required to use the Password Reset Post Challenge trigger.

If provided, organization parameters will be made available to the email template and organization branding will be applied to the prompt. In addition, the redirect link in the prompt will include organization_id and organization_name query string parameters.

new_password.description: Password to set for the user once the ticket is used.

ttl_sec.description: Number of seconds for which the ticket is valid before expiration. If unspecified or set to 0, this value defaults to 432000 seconds (5 days).

callbacks.description: Comma-separated list of URLs whitelisted for Auth0 to use as a callback to the client after authentication.

allowed_origins.description: Comma-separated list of URLs allowed to make requests from JavaScript to Auth0 API (typically used with CORS). By default, all your callback URLs will be allowed. This field allows you to enter other origins if necessary. You can also use wildcards at the subdomain level (e.g., https://*.contoso.com). Query strings and hash information are not taken into account when validating these URLs.

web_origins.description: Comma-separated list of allowed origins for use with Cross-Origin Authentication, Device Flow, and web message response mode.

client_aliases.description: List of audiences/realms for SAML protocol. Used by the wsfed addon.

allowed_clients.description: List of allow clients and API ids that are allowed to make delegation requests. Empty means all all your clients are allowed.

allowed_logout_urls.description: Comma-separated list of URLs that are valid to redirect to after logout from Auth0. Wildcards are allowed for subdomains.

grant_types.description: List of grant types supported for this application. Can include `authorization_code`, `implicit`, `refresh_token`, `client_credentials`, `password`, `http://auth0.com/oauth/grant-type/password-realm`, `http://auth0.com/oauth/grant-type/mfa-oob`, `http://auth0.com/oauth/grant-type/mfa-otp`, `http://auth0.com/oauth/grant-type/mfa-recovery-code`, `urn:openid:params:grant-type:ciba`, `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code`, and `urn:auth0:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange:federated-connection-access-token`.

sso.description: Applies only to SSO clients and determines whether Auth0 will handle Single Sign On (true) or whether the Identity Provider will (false).

sso_disabled.description: Whether Single Sign On is disabled (true) or enabled (true). Defaults to true.

cross_origin_authentication.description: Whether this client can be used to make cross-origin authentication requests (true) or it is not allowed to make such requests (false).

cross_origin_auth.description: Whether this client can be used to make cross-origin authentication requests (true) or it is not allowed to make such requests (false).

cross_origin_loc.description: URL of the location in your site where the cross origin verification takes place for the cross-origin auth flow when performing Auth in your own domain instead of Auth0 hosted login page.

custom_login_page_on.description: Whether a custom login page is to be used (true) or the default provided login page (false).

custom_login_page.description: The content (HTML, CSS, JS) of the custom login page.

custom_login_page_preview.description: The content (HTML, CSS, JS) of the custom login page. (Used on Previews)

form_template.description: HTML form template to be used for WS-Federation.

is_token_endpoint_ip_header_trusted.description: If true, trust that the IP specified in the `auth0-forwarded-for` header is the end-user's IP for brute-force-protection on token endpoint.

initiate_login_uri.description: Initiate login uri, must be https

organization_discovery_methods.description: Defines the available methods for organization discovery during the `pre_login_prompt`. Users can discover their organization either by `email`, `organization_name` or both.

require_pushed_authorization_requests.description: Makes the use of Pushed Authorization Requests mandatory for this client

require_proof_of_possession.description: Makes the use of Proof-of-Possession mandatory for this client

skip_non_verifiable_callback_uri_confirm.description:
Controls whether a confirmation prompt is shown during login flows when the redirect URI uses non-verifiable callback URIs (for example, a custom URI schema such as `myapp://`, or `localhost`).
If set to true, a confirmation prompt will not be shown. We recommend that this is set to false for improved protection from malicious apps.
See https://auth0.com/docs/secure/security-guidance/measures-against-app-impersonation for more information.

par_request_expiry.description: Specifies how long, in seconds, a Pushed Authorization Request URI remains valid

resource_server_identifier.description: The identifier of the resource server that this client is linked to.

external_client_id.description: An alternate client identifier to be used during authorization flows. Only supports CIMD-based client identifiers.

jwks_uri.description: URL for the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) containing the public keys used for private_key_jwt authentication. Only present for CIMD clients using private_key_jwt authentication.

accountName.description: Your Azure storage account name. Usually first segment in your Azure storage URL. e.g. `https://acme-org.blob.core.windows.net` would be the account name `acme-org`.

storageAccessKey.description: Access key associated with this storage account.

containerName.description: Container to request a token for. e.g. `my-container`.

blobName.description: Entity to request a token for. e.g. `my-blob`. If blank the computed SAS will apply to the entire storage container.

expiration.description: Expiration in minutes for the generated token (default of 5 minutes).

signedIdentifier.description: Shared access policy identifier defined in your storage account resource.

blob_read.description: Indicates if the issued token has permission to read the content, properties, metadata and block list. Use the blob as the source of a copy operation.

blob_write.description: Indicates if the issued token has permission to create or write content, properties, metadata, or block list. Snapshot or lease the blob. Resize the blob (page blob only). Use the blob as the destination of a copy operation within the same account.

blob_delete.description: Indicates if the issued token has permission to delete the blob.

container_read.description: Indicates if the issued token has permission to read the content, properties, metadata or block list of any blob in the container. Use any blob in the container as the source of a copy operation

container_write.description: Indicates that for any blob in the container if the issued token has permission to create or write content, properties, metadata, or block list. Snapshot or lease the blob. Resize the blob (page blob only). Use the blob as the destination of a copy operation within the same account.

container_delete.description: Indicates if issued token has permission to delete any blob in the container.

container_list.description: Indicates if the issued token has permission to list blobs in the container.

namespace.description: Your Azure Service Bus namespace. Usually the first segment of your Service Bus URL (e.g. `https://acme-org.servicebus.windows.net` would be `acme-org`).

sasKeyName.description: Your shared access policy name defined in your Service Bus entity.

sasKey.description: Primary Key associated with your shared access policy.

entityPath.description: Entity you want to request a token for. e.g. `my-queue`.'

expiration.description: Optional expiration in minutes for the generated token. Defaults to 5 minutes.

ClientAddonBox.description: Box SSO indicator (no configuration settings needed for Box SSO).

ClientAddonCloudBees.description: CloudBees SSO indicator (no configuration settings needed for CloudBees SSO).

ClientAddonConcur.description: Concur SSO indicator (no configuration settings needed for Concur SSO).

ClientAddonDropbox.description: Dropbox SSO indicator (no configuration settings needed for Dropbox SSO).

domain.description: Your custom domain found in your EchoSign URL. e.g. `https://acme-org.echosign.com` would be `acme-org`.
Your custom domain found in your Egnyte URL. e.g. `https://acme-org.egnyte.com` would be `acme-org`.

private_key_id.description: Optional ID of the private key to obtain kid header in the issued token (SDK v3+ tokens only).

private_key.description: Private Key for signing the token (SDK v3+ tokens only).

client_email.description: ID of the Service Account you have created (shown as `client_email` in the generated JSON file, SDK v3+ tokens only).

lifetime_in_seconds.description: Optional expiration in seconds for the generated token. Defaults to 3600 seconds (SDK v3+ tokens only).

providerId.description: Provider ID of your Layer account

keyId.description: Authentication Key identifier used to sign the Layer token.

privateKey.description: Private key for signing the Layer token.

principal.description: Name of the property used as the unique user id in Layer. If not specified `user_id` is used.

expiration.description: Optional expiration in minutes for the generated token. Defaults to 5 minutes.

account.description: Your New Relic Account ID found in your New Relic URL after the `/accounts/` path. e.g. `https://rpm.newrelic.com/accounts/123456/query` would be `123456`.

connection.description: Optional Auth0 database connection for testing an already-configured Office 365 tenant.

ClientAddonRMS.description: Active Directory Rights Management Service SSO configuration.

url.description: URL of your Rights Management Server. It can be internal or external, but users will have to be able to reach it.

ClientAddonSAML.description: SAML2 addon indicator (no configuration settings needed for SAML2 addon).

clientid.description: If activated in the OAuth 2.0 client configuration (transaction SOAUTH2) the SAML attribute client_id must be set and equal the client_id form parameter of the access token request.

usernameAttribute.description: Name of the property in the user object that maps to a SAP username. e.g. `email`.

tokenEndpointUrl.description: Your SAP OData server OAuth2 token endpoint URL.

servicePassword.description: Service account password to use to authenticate API calls to the token endpoint.

nameIdentifierFormat.description: NameID element of the Subject which can be used to express the user's identity. Defaults to `urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified`.

entity_id.description: Arbitrary logical URL that identifies the Saleforce resource. e.g. `https://acme-org.com`.

clientid.description: Consumer Key assigned by Salesforce to the Connected App.

principal.description: Name of the property in the user object that maps to a Salesforce username. e.g. `email`.

clientid.description: Consumer Key assigned by Salesforce to the Connected App.

principal.description: Name of the property in the user object that maps to a Salesforce username. e.g. `email`.

org_slug.description: Generated slug for your Sentry organization. Found in your Sentry URL. e.g. `https://sentry.acme.com/acme-org/` would be `acme-org`.

base_url.description: URL prefix only if running Sentry Community Edition, otherwise leave should be blank.

ClientAddonSharePointExternalURL.description: External SharePoint application URLs if exposed to the Internet.

ClientAddonSlack.description: Slack team or workspace name usually first segment in your Slack URL. e.g. `https://acme-org.slack.com` would be `acme-org`.

ClientAddonWAMS.description: Windows Azure Mobile Services addon configuration.

masterkey.description: Your master key for Windows Azure Mobile Services.

ClientAddonWSFed.description: WS-Fed (WIF) addon indicator. Actual configuration is stored in `callback` and `client_aliases` properties on the client.

accountName.description: Zendesk account name usually first segment in your Zendesk URL. e.g. `https://acme-org.zendesk.com` would be `acme-org`.

account.description: Zoom account name usually first segment of your Zoom URL, e.g. `https://acme-org.zoom.us` would be `acme-org`.

ClientAddons.description: Addons enabled for this client and their associated configurations.

ClientAppTypeEnum.description: The type of application this client represents

ClientAsyncApprovalNotificationsChannels.description: Array of notification channels for contacting the user when their approval is required. Valid values are `guardian-push`, `email`.
Array of notification channels for contacting the user when their approval is required. Valid values are `guardian-push`, `email`.

ClientAuthenticationMethodPrivateKeyJWT.description: Defines `private_key_jwt` client authentication method. If this property is defined, the client is enabled to use the Private Key JWT authentication method.

ClientAuthenticationMethodPrivateKeyJWTC.description: A list of unique and previously created credential IDs enabled on the client for Private Key JWT authentication.

ClientAuthenticationMethodSelfSignedTLSC.description: Defines `self_signed_tls_client_auth` client authentication method. If the property is defined, the client is configured to use mTLS authentication method utilizing self-signed certificate.
A list of unique and previously created credential IDs enabled on the client for mTLS authentication utilizing self-signed certificate.

ClientAuthenticationMethodTLSClientAuthC.description: A list of unique and previously created credential IDs enabled on the client for CA-based mTLS authentication.

ClientComplianceLevelEnum.description: Defines the compliance level for this client, which may restrict it's capabilities

Fully defined credentials that will be enabled on the client for Private Key JWT authentication.

ClientCreateAuthenticationMethodTLSClien.description: Defines `tls_client_auth` client authentication method. If the property is defined, the client is configured to use CA-based mTLS authentication method.
Fully defined credentials that will be enabled on the client for CA-based mTLS authentication.

id.description: ID of the credential. Generated on creation.

name.description: The name given to the credential by the user.

kid.description: The key identifier of the credential, generated on creation.

subject_dn.description: The X509 certificate's Subject Distinguished Name

created_at.description: The ISO 8601 formatted date the credential was created.

updated_at.description: The ISO 8601 formatted date the credential was updated.

expires_at.description: The ISO 8601 formatted date representing the expiration of the credential.

ClientCredentialAlgorithmEnum.description: Algorithm which will be used with the credential. Supported algorithms: RS256,RS384,PS256

organization_id.description: The default Organization ID to be used

ClientEncryptionKey.description: Encryption used for WsFed responses with this client.

cert.description: Encryption certificate for public key in X.509 (.CER) format.

subject.description: Encryption certificate name for this certificate in the format `/CN={domain}`.

ClientExternalMetadataCreatedByEnum.description: Indicates who created the external metadata client. The value admin indicates the client was registered via the Management API. The value client indicates the client was registered dynamically. This field is only present when external_metadata_type is set.

ClientExternalMetadataTypeEnum.description: Indicates the type of external metadata used to register the client. This field is omitted for regular clients. The value cimd identifies clients registered via a Client ID Metadata Document.

ClientGrantDefaultForEnum.description: Used to filter the returned client grants to include only default client grants for the specified group of clients.

ClientGrantOrganizationNullableUsageEnum.description: Controls how organizations may be used with this grant

ClientGrantOrganizationUsageEnum.description: Defines whether organizations can be used with client credentials exchanges for this grant.

audience.description: The audience (API identifier) of this client grant.

scope.description: Scopes allowed for this client grant.

allow_any_organization.description: If enabled, any organization can be used with this grant. If disabled (default), the grant must be explicitly assigned to the desired organizations.

default_for.description: Specifies that the client grant is the default for the target audience (API identifier) for the specified group of clients. The only permissible value is `third_party_clients`.

is_system.description: If enabled, this grant is a special grant created by Auth0. It cannot be modified or deleted directly.

authorization_details_types.description: Types of authorization_details allowed for this client grant.

allow_all_scopes.description: If enabled, all scopes configured on the resource server are allowed for this grant.

ClientGrantSubjectTypeEnum.description: The type of application access the client grant allows.

secret_encoded.description: Whether the client secret is base64 encoded (true) or unencoded (false).

sha256_cert_fingerprints.description: SHA256 fingerprints of the app's signing certificate. Multiple fingerprints can be used to support different versions of your app, such as debug and production builds.

team_id.description: Identifier assigned to the Apple account that signs and uploads the app to the store.

app_bundle_identifier.description: Assigned by developer to the app as its unique identifier inside the store. Usually this is a reverse domain plus the app name, e.g. `com.you.MyApp`.

ClientOIDCBackchannelLogoutInitiators.description: Configuration for OIDC backchannel logout initiators

ClientOIDCBackchannelLogoutInitiatorsEnu.description: The `selected_initiators` property contains the list of initiators to be enabled for the given application.

ClientOIDCBackchannelLogoutInitiatorsMod.description: The `mode` property determines the configuration method for enabling initiators. `custom` enables only the initiators listed in the selected_initiators array, `all` enables all current and future initiators.

ClientOIDCBackchannelLogoutSessionMetada.description: Controls whether session metadata is included in the logout token. Default value is null.

include.description: The `include` property determines whether session metadata is included in the logout token.

backchannel_logout_urls.description: Comma-separated list of URLs that are valid to call back from Auth0 for OIDC backchannel logout. Currently only one URL is allowed.

ClientOrganizationDiscoveryEnum.description: Method for discovering organizations during the `pre_login_prompt`. `email` allows users to find their organization by entering their email address and performing domain matching, while `organization_name` requires users to enter the organization name directly. These methods can be combined.

ClientOrganizationRequireBehaviorEnum.description: Defines how to proceed during an authentication transaction when `client.organization_usage: 'require'`. Can be `no_prompt` (default), `pre_login_prompt` or `post_login_prompt`. `post_login_prompt` requires `oidc_conformant: true`.

ClientOrganizationRequireBehaviorPatchEn.description: Defines how to proceed during an authentication transaction when `client.organization_usage: 'require'`. Can be `no_prompt` (default), `pre_login_prompt` or `post_login_prompt`. `post_login_prompt` requires `oidc_conformant: true`.

ClientOrganizationUsageEnum.description: Defines how to proceed during an authentication transaction with regards an organization. Can be `deny` (default), `allow` or `require`.

ClientOrganizationUsagePatchEnum.description: Defines how to proceed during an authentication transaction with regards an organization. Can be `deny` (default), `allow` or `require`.

ClientRedirectionPolicyEnum.description: Controls whether users are automatically redirected to the application's callback URL when errors occur during authentication, or in Email redirect links. When set to 'open_redirect_protection', an error page is shown instead of redirecting. Use 'open_redirect_protection' for untrusted applications to prevent open redirects.

leeway.description: Period in seconds where the previous refresh token can be exchanged without triggering breach detection

token_lifetime.description: Period (in seconds) for which refresh tokens will remain valid

infinite_token_lifetime.description: Prevents tokens from having a set lifetime when `true` (takes precedence over `token_lifetime` values)

idle_token_lifetime.description: Period (in seconds) for which refresh tokens will remain valid without use

infinite_idle_token_lifetime.description: Prevents tokens from expiring without use when `true` (takes precedence over `idle_token_lifetime` values)

policies.description: A collection of policies governing multi-resource refresh token exchange (MRRT), defining how refresh tokens can be used across different resource servers

audience.description: The identifier of the resource server to which the Multi Resource Refresh Token Policy applies

scope.description: The resource server permissions granted under the Multi Resource Refresh Token Policy, defining the context in which an access token can be used

items.description: A resource server permission granted under the Multi Resource Refresh Token Policy

identifier.description: The resource server identifier. It is recommended to use a URL for this

scopes.description: The scopes to request from the user to access the resource server

items.description: A scope to request from the user to access the resource server

can_create_session_transfer_token.description: Indicates whether an app can issue a Session Transfer Token through Token Exchange. If set to 'false', the app will not be able to issue a Session Transfer Token. Usually configured in the native application. Default value is `false`.

enforce_cascade_revocation.description: Indicates whether revoking the parent Refresh Token that initiated a Native to Web flow and was used to issue a Session Transfer Token should trigger a cascade revocation affecting its dependent child entities. Usually configured in the native application. Default value is `true`, applicable only in Native to Web SSO context.

allowed_authentication_methods.description: Indicates whether an app can create a session from a Session Transfer Token received via indicated methods. Can include `cookie` and/or `query`. Usually configured in the web application. Default value is an empty array [].

allow_refresh_token.description: Indicates whether Refresh Tokens are allowed to be issued when authenticating with a Session Transfer Token. Usually configured in the web application. Default value is `false`.

enforce_online_refresh_tokens.description: Indicates whether Refresh Tokens created during a Native to Web session are tied to that session's lifetime. This determines if such refresh tokens should be automatically revoked when their corresponding sessions are. Usually configured in the web application. Default value is `true`, applicable only in Native to Web SSO context.

ClientSessionTransferDeviceBindingEnum.description: Indicates whether device binding security should be enforced for the app. If set to 'ip', the app will enforce device binding by IP, meaning that consumption of Session Transfer Token must be done from the same IP of the issuer. Likewise, if set to 'asn', device binding is enforced by ASN, meaning consumption of Session Transfer Token must be done from the same ASN as the issuer. If set to 'none', device binding is not enforced. Usually configured in the web application. Default value is `ip`.

required.description: Indicates whether the JAR requests are mandatory
Indicates whether the JAR requests are mandatory

pkcs7.description: Signing certificate public key and chain in PKCS#7 (.P7B) format.

cert.description: Signing certificate public key in X.509 (.CER) format.

subject.description: Subject name for this certificate in the format `/CN={domain}`.

ClientSigningKeys.description: Signing certificates associated with this client.

ClientThirdPartySecurityModeEnum.description: Security mode for third-party clients. Controls which properties and features are allowed. Set to `strict` for enhanced security and functionality (recommended for new clients) or `permissive` if broader access is required. Only applies when `is_first_party` is `false`.

ClientTokenEndpointAuthMethodEnum.description: Defines the requested authentication method for the token endpoint. Can be `none` (public client without a client secret), `client_secret_post` (client uses HTTP POST parameters), or `client_secret_basic` (client uses HTTP Basic).

ClientTokenEndpointAuthMethodOrNullEnum.description: Defines the requested authentication method for the token endpoint. Can be `none` (public client without a client secret), `client_secret_post` (client uses HTTP POST parameters), or `client_secret_basic` (client uses HTTP Basic).

allow_any_profile_of_type.description: List the enabled token exchange types for this client.

ConnectionApiEnableUsersGoogleApps.description: Enables integration with the Google Workspace Admin SDK Directory API. When true, Auth0 can retrieve extended user attributes (admin status, suspension status, group memberships) and supports inbound directory provisioning (SCIM). Defaults to true.

ConnectionAuthParamsMap.description: Maps parameter names from Auth0's /authorize endpoint to the identity provider's authorization endpoint parameters. For example, mapping 'audience' to 'resource' transforms the parameter name during authorization requests. Applied after authParams merging. See https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/identity-providers/social-identity-providers/oauth2#pass-dynamic-parameters

ConnectionAuthParamsOAuth1.description: Additional static parameters included in every authorization request to the identity provider. These parameters are merged with runtime parameters before the authorization redirect. Keys and values are passed as-is to the identity provider's authorization endpoint.

ConnectionAuthParamsOAuth2.description: Additional static parameters included in every authorization request to the identity provider. These parameters are merged with runtime parameters before the authorization redirect. Keys and values are passed as-is to the identity provider's authorization endpoint. See https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/identity-providers/social-identity-providers/oauth2#pass-static-parameters

ConnectionAuthenticationPurpose.description: Configure the purpose of a connection to be used for authentication during login.

allOf.description: URL of the identity provider's OAuth 2.0 authorization endpoint where users are redirected for authentication. Must be a valid HTTPS URL. This endpoint initiates the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow.

ConnectionBruteForceProtection.description: Enables Auth0's brute force protection to prevent credential stuffing attacks. When enabled, blocks suspicious login attempts from specific IP addresses after repeated failures.

ConnectionCertRolloverNotification.description: Timestamp last cert-rollover-service notification was sent. Should not be set by customers.

ConnectionCertsAD.description: Array of X.509 certificates in PEM format used for validating SAML signatures from the AD identity provider (managed by the AD Connector agent).

ConnectionClaimTypesSupported.description: JSON array containing a list of the Claim Types that the OpenID Provider supports. These Claim Types are described in Section 5.6 of OpenID Connect Core 1.0 [OpenID.Core]. If omitted, the implementation supports only normal Claims.

ConnectionClaimsLocalesSupported.description: Languages and scripts supported for values in Claims being returned, represented as a JSON array of BCP47 [RFC5646] language tag values. Not all languages and scripts are necessarily supported for all Claim values.

basic_profile.description: Includes basic user profile information from Azure AD (name, email, given_name, family_name). Always enabled and required - represents the minimum profile data retrieved during authentication.

ext_access_token.description: When false, prevents storing the user's Azure AD access token in the Auth0 user profile. When true (default), the access token is persisted for API access.

ext_account_enabled.description: When false, prevents storing whether the user's Azure AD account is enabled. When true (default), the account enabled status is persisted in the user profile.

ext_assigned_licenses.description: When false, prevents storing the list of Microsoft 365/Office 365 licenses assigned to the user. When true (default), license information is persisted in the user profile.

ext_azure_id.description: When false, prevents storing the user's Azure ID identifier. When true (default), the Azure ID is persisted. Note: 'oid' (Object ID) is the recommended unique identifier for single-tenant connections.

ext_city.description: When false, prevents storing the user's city from Azure AD. When true (default), city information is persisted in the user profile.

ext_country.description: When false, prevents storing the user's country from Azure AD. When true (default), country information is persisted in the user profile.

ext_department.description: When false, prevents storing the user's department from Azure AD. When true (default), department information is persisted in the user profile.

ext_dir_sync_enabled.description: When false, prevents storing whether directory synchronization is enabled for the user. When true (default), directory sync status is persisted in the user profile.

ext_email.description: When false, prevents storing the user's email address from Azure AD. When true (default), email is persisted in the user profile.

ext_expires_in.description: When false, prevents storing the token expiration time (in seconds). When true (default), expiration information is persisted in the user profile.

ext_family_name.description: When false, prevents storing the user's family name (last name) from Azure AD. When true (default), family name is persisted in the user profile.

ext_fax.description: When false, prevents storing the user's fax number from Azure AD. When true (default), fax information is persisted in the user profile.

ext_given_name.description: When false, prevents storing the user's given name (first name) from Azure AD. When true (default), given name is persisted in the user profile.

ext_group_ids.description: When false, prevents storing the list of Azure AD group IDs the user is a member of. When true (default), group membership IDs are persisted. See ext_groups for retrieving group details.

ext_job_title.description: When false, prevents storing the user's job title from Azure AD. When true (default), job title information is persisted in the user profile.

ext_last_sync.description: When false, prevents storing the timestamp of the last directory synchronization. When true (default), the last sync date is persisted in the user profile.

ext_mobile.description: When false, prevents storing the user's mobile phone number from Azure AD. When true (default), mobile number is persisted in the user profile.

ext_name.description: When false, prevents storing the user's full name from Azure AD. When true (default), full name is persisted in the user profile.

ext_nested_groups.description: When true, stores all groups the user is member of, including transitive group memberships (groups within groups). When false (default), only direct group memberships are included.

ext_nickname.description: When false, prevents storing the user's nickname or display name from Azure AD. When true (default), nickname is persisted in the user profile.

ext_oid.description: When false, prevents storing the user's Object ID (oid) from Azure AD. When true (default), the oid is persisted. Note: 'oid' is the recommended unique identifier for single-tenant connections and required for SCIM.

ext_phone.description: When false, prevents storing the user's phone number from Azure AD. When true (default), phone number is persisted in the user profile.

ext_physical_delivery_office_name.description: When false, prevents storing the user's office location from Azure AD. When true (default), office location is persisted in the user profile.

ext_postal_code.description: When false, prevents storing the user's postal code from Azure AD. When true (default), postal code is persisted in the user profile.

ext_preferred_language.description: When false, prevents storing the user's preferred language from Azure AD. When true (default), language preference is persisted in the user profile.

ext_provisioned_plans.description: When false, prevents storing the list of service plans provisioned to the user. When true (default), provisioned plans are persisted in the user profile.

ext_provisioning_errors.description: When false, prevents storing provisioning errors that occurred during synchronization. When true (default), error information is persisted. Useful for troubleshooting sync issues.

ext_proxy_addresses.description: When false, prevents storing all proxy email addresses (email aliases) for the user. When true (default), proxy addresses are persisted in the user profile.

ext_puid.description: When false, prevents storing the user's Passport User ID (puid). When true (default), puid is persisted in the user profile. Legacy attribute.

ext_refresh_token.description: When false, prevents storing the Azure AD refresh token. When true (default), the refresh token is persisted for offline access. Required for token refresh in long-lived applications.

ext_roles.description: When false, prevents storing Azure AD application roles assigned to the user. When true (default), role information is persisted. Useful for RBAC in applications.

ext_state.description: When false, prevents storing the user's state (province/region) from Azure AD. When true (default), state information is persisted in the user profile.

ext_street.description: When false, prevents storing the user's street address from Azure AD. When true (default), street address is persisted in the user profile.

ext_telephoneNumber.description: When false, prevents storing the user's telephone number from Azure AD. When true (default), telephone number is persisted in the user profile.

ext_tenantid.description: When false, prevents storing the user's Azure AD tenant ID. When true (default), tenant ID is persisted. Useful for identifying which Azure AD organization the user belongs to.

ext_upn.description: When false, prevents storing the user's User Principal Name (UPN) from Azure AD. When true (default), UPN is persisted. UPN is the user's logon name (e.g., user@contoso.com).

ext_usage_location.description: When false, prevents storing the user's usage location for license assignment. When true (default), usage location is persisted in the user profile.

ext_user_id.description: When false, prevents storing an alternative user ID. When true (default), this user ID is persisted in the user profile.

granted.description: Indicates whether admin consent has been granted for the required Azure AD permissions. Read-only status field managed by Auth0 during the OAuth authorization flow.

use_wsfed.description: Indicates WS-Federation protocol usage. When true, uses WS-Federation; when false, uses OpenID Connect.

ConnectionOptionsClientIdGithub.description: OAuth 2.0 client identifier issued by the identity provider during application registration. This value identifies your Auth0 connection to the identity provider.

anyOf.description: relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

ConnectionOptionsClientIdTwitter.description: OAuth 2.0 client identifier issued by the identity provider during application registration. This value identifies your Auth0 connection to the identity provider.

anyOf.description: relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

ConnectionOptionsClientSecretGithub.description: OAuth 2.0 client secret issued by the identity provider during application registration. This value is used to authenticate your Auth0 connection to the identity provider.

anyOf.description: relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

ConnectionOptionsClientSecretTwitter.description: OAuth 2.0 client secret issued by the identity provider during application registration. This value is used to authenticate your Auth0 connection to the identity provider.

anyOf.description: relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

ConnectionOptionsClientTypeEnumTwitter.description: Specifies whether the Twitter application is a confidential client (able to protect its credentials) or a public client (running in an environment such as mobile or SPA where secrets cannot be stored safely).

ConnectionOptionsCommon.description: Common attributes for connection options including non-persistent attributes and Cross App Access

ConnectionOptionsCommonSAML.description: Common options for SAML-based enterprise connections (shared by samlp and pingfederate).

expires.description: ISO 8601 formatted datetime indicating when the identity provider's signing certificate expires. Automatically extracted from the certificate's validity during connection creation/update. Used for certificate expiration tracking and warnings.

ConnectionOptionsDeflateSAML.description: When true, enables DEFLATE compression for SAML requests sent via HTTP-Redirect binding.

anyOf.description: relaxed validation to allow data already in the database

ConnectionOptionsEvernote.description: Options for the evernote family of connections

profile.description: Enables retrieval of basic profile attributes from Exact Online including name, username, picture, email, gender, and language.

graphAPIVersion.description: The version of the Facebook Graph API to use for authentication requests.

ads_management.description: Grants permission to both read and manage ads for ad accounts you own or have been granted access to by the owner. By default, your app may only access ad accounts owned by admins of the app when in developer mode.

ads_read.description: Grants access to the Ads Insights API to pull ads report information for ad accounts you own or have been granted access to by the owner of other ad accounts.

allow_context_profile_field.description: Provides access to a social context. Deprecated on April 30th, 2019.

business_management.description: Grants permission to read and write with the Business Manager API.

email.description: Grants permission to access a person's primary email address.

groups_access_member_info.description: Grants permission to publicly available group member information.

leads_retrieval.description: Grants permission to retrieve all the information captured within a lead.

manage_notifications.description: Enables your app to read a person's notifications and mark them as read. This permission does not let you send notifications to a person. Deprecated in Graph API v2.3.

manage_pages.description: Grants permission to retrieve Page Access Tokens for the Pages and Apps that the person administers. Apps need both manage_pages and publish_pages to be able to publish as a Page.

pages_manage_cta.description: Allows the app to perform POST and DELETE operations on endpoints used for managing a Page's Call To Action buttons.

pages_manage_instant_articles.description: Grants permission to manage Instant Articles on behalf of Facebook Pages administered by people using your app.

pages_messaging.description: Grants permission to send and receive messages through a Facebook Page.

pages_messaging_phone_number.description: Grants permission to use the phone number messaging feature.

pages_messaging_subscriptions.description: Grants permission to send messages using Facebook Pages at any time after the first user interaction. Your app may only send advertising or promotional content through sponsored messages or within 24 hours of user interaction.

pages_show_list.description: Grants access to show the list of the Pages that a person manages.

public_profile.description: Provides access to a user's public profile information including id, first_name, last_name, middle_name, name, name_format, picture, and short_name. This is the most basic permission and is required by Facebook.

publish_actions.description: Allows your app to publish to the Open Graph using Built-in Actions, Achievements, Scores, or Custom Actions. Deprecated on August 1st, 2018.

publish_pages.description: Grants permission to publish posts, comments, and like Pages managed by a person using your app. Your app must also have manage_pages to publish as a Page.

publish_to_groups.description: Grants permission to post content into a group on behalf of a user who has granted the app this permission.

publish_video.description: Grants permission to publish live videos to the app User's timeline.

read_audience_network_insights.description: Grants read-only access to the Audience Network Insights data for Apps the person owns.

read_insights.description: Grants read-only access to the Insights data for Pages, Apps, and web domains the person owns.

read_mailbox.description: Provides the ability to read the messages in a person's Facebook Inbox through the inbox edge and the thread node. Deprecated in Graph API v2.3.

read_page_mailboxes.description: Grants permission to read from the Page Inboxes of the Pages managed by a person. This permission is often used alongside the manage_pages permission.

read_stream.description: Provides access to read the posts in a person's News Feed, or the posts on their Profile. Deprecated in Graph API v2.3.

user_age_range.description: Grants permission to access a person's age range.

user_birthday.description: Grants permission to access a person's birthday.

user_events.description: Grants read-only access to the Events a person is a host of or has RSVPed to. This permission is restricted to a limited set of partners and usage requires prior approval by Facebook.

user_friends.description: Grants permission to access a list of friends that also use said app. This permission is restricted to a limited set of partners and usage requires prior approval by Facebook.

user_gender.description: Grants permission to access a person's gender.

user_groups.description: Enables your app to read the Groups a person is a member of through the groups edge on the User object. Deprecated in Graph API v2.3.

user_hometown.description: Grants permission to access a person's hometown location set in their User Profile.

user_likes.description: Grants permission to access the list of all Facebook Pages that a person has liked.

user_link.description: Grants permission to access the Facebook Profile URL of the user of your app.

user_location.description: Provides access to a person's current city through the location field on the User object. The current city is set by a person on their Profile.

user_managed_groups.description: Enables your app to read the Groups a person is an admin of through the groups edge on the User object. Deprecated in Graph API v3.0.

user_photos.description: Provides access to the photos a person has uploaded or been tagged in. This permission is restricted to a limited set of partners and usage requires prior approval by Facebook.

user_posts.description: Provides access to the posts on a person's Timeline including their own posts, posts they are tagged in, and posts other people make on their Timeline. This permission is restricted to a limited set of partners and usage requires prior approval by Facebook.

user_status.description: Provides access to a person's statuses. These are posts on Facebook which don't include links, videos or photos. Deprecated in Graph API v2.3.

user_tagged_places.description: Provides access to the Places a person has been tagged at in photos, videos, statuses and links. This permission is restricted to a limited set of partners and usage requires prior approval by Facebook.

user_videos.description: Provides access to the videos a person has uploaded or been tagged in. This permission is restricted to a limited set of partners and usage requires prior approval by Facebook.

ConnectionOptionsFreeformScopesGithub.description: Array of custom OAuth 2.0 scopes to request from GitHub during authentication. Use this to request scopes not covered by the predefined scope options.

anyOf.description: relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

admin_org.description: Requests the GitHub admin:org scope so Auth0 can fully manage organizations, teams, and memberships on behalf of the user.

admin_public_key.description: Requests the admin:public_key scope to allow creating, updating, and deleting the user's SSH public keys.

admin_repo_hook.description: Requests the admin:repo_hook scope so Auth0 can read, write, ping, and delete repository webhooks.

delete_repo.description: Requests the delete_repo scope so the user can remove repositories they administer while signing in through Auth0.

email.description: Requests the user:email scope so Auth0 pulls addresses from GitHub's /user/emails endpoint and populates the profile.

follow.description: Requests the user:follow scope to allow following or unfollowing GitHub users for the signed-in account.

gist.description: Requests the gist scope so the application can create or update gists on behalf of the user.

notifications.description: Requests the notifications scope to read GitHub inbox notifications; repo also implicitly grants this access.

profile.description: Controls the GitHub read:user call that returns the user's basic profile (name, avatar, profile URL) and is on by default for successful logins.

public_repo.description: Requests the public_repo scope for read and write operations on public repositories, deployments, and statuses.

read_org.description: Requests the read:org scope so Auth0 can view organizations, teams, and membership lists without making changes.

read_public_key.description: Requests the read:public_key scope so Auth0 can list and inspect the user's SSH public keys.

read_repo_hook.description: Requests the read:repo_hook scope to read and ping repository webhooks.

read_user.description: Requests the read:user scope to load extended profile information, implicitly covering user:email and user:follow.

repo.description: Requests the repo scope for read and write access to both public and private repositories, deployments, and statuses.

repo_deployment.description: Requests the repo_deployment scope in order to read and write deployment statuses for repositories.

repo_status.description: Requests the repo:status scope to manage commit statuses on public and private repositories.

write_org.description: Requests the write:org scope so Auth0 can change whether organization memberships are publicized.

write_public_key.description: Requests the write:public_key scope to create or update SSH public keys for the user.

write_repo_hook.description: Requests the write:repo_hook scope so Auth0 can read, create, update, and ping repository webhooks.

allow_setting_login_scopes.description: When true, allows customization of OAuth scopes requested during user login. Custom scopes are appended to the mandatory email and profile scopes. When false or omitted, only the default email and profile scopes are used. This property is automatically enabled when Token Vault or Connected Accounts features are activated.

email.description: Whether the OAuth flow requests the `email` scope.

ext_groups_extended.description: Controls whether enriched group entries include `id`, `email`, `name` (true) or only the group name (false); can only be set when `ext_groups` is true.

map_user_id_to_id.description: Determines how Auth0 generates the user_id for Google Workspace users. When false (default), the user's email address is used. When true, Google's stable numeric user ID is used instead, which persists even if the user's email changes. This setting can only be configured when creating the connection and cannot be changed afterward.

profile.description: Whether the OAuth flow requests the `profile` scope.

adsense_management.description: View and manage user's ad applications, ad units, and channels in AdSense

analytics.description: View user's configuration information and reports

blogger.description: View and manage user's posts and blogs on Blogger and Blogger comments

calendar.description: See, edit, share, and permanently delete all the calendars you can access using Google Calendar

calendar_events.description: View and edit events on all your calendars

calendar_events_readonly.description: View events on all your calendars

calendar_read.description: See and download any calendar you can access using your Calendar

chrome_web_store.description: Read access to user's chrome web store

contacts.description: Full access to the authenticated user's contacts

contacts_new.description: Full access to the authenticated user's contacts

contacts_other_readonly.description: Read-only access to the authenticated user's 'Other contacts'

contacts_readonly.description: Read-only access to the authenticated user's contacts

content_api_for_shopping.description: View and manage user's products, feeds, and subaccounts

coordinate.description: Grants read and write access to the Coordinate API

coordinate_readonly.description: Grants read access to the Coordinate API

directory_readonly.description: Read-only access to the authenticated user's corporate directory (if applicable)

document_list.description: Access to Google Docs document list feed

drive.description: Full access to all files and folders in the user's Google Drive

drive_activity.description: View and add to the activity record of files in your Drive

drive_activity_readonly.description: View the activity record of files in your Drive

drive_appdata.description: Access to the application's configuration data in the user's Google Drive

drive_apps_readonly.description: View apps authorized to access your Drive

drive_file.description: Access to files created or opened by the app

drive_metadata.description: Access to file metadata, including listing files and folders

drive_photos_readonly.description: Read-only access to the user's Google Photos

drive_readonly.description: Read-only access to all files and folders in the user's Google Drive

drive_scripts.description: Modify the behavior of Google Apps Scripts

email.description: Email and verified email flag

gmail.description: Full access to the account's mailboxes, including permanent deletion of threads and messages

gmail_compose.description: Read all resources and their metadata—no write operations

gmail_labels.description: Create, read, update, and delete labels only

gmail_metadata.description: Read resources metadata including labels, history records, and email message headers, but not the message body or attachments

gmail_modify.description: All read/write operations except immediate, permanent deletion of threads and messages, bypassing Trash

gmail_new.description: Full access to the account's mailboxes, including permanent deletion of threads and messages

gmail_readonly.description: Read all resources and their metadata—no write operations

gmail_send.description: Send messages only. No read or modify privileges on mailbox

gmail_settings_sharing.description: Manage sensitive mail settings, including forwarding rules and aliases. Note: Operations guarded by this scope are restricted to administrative use only

google_affiliate_network.description: View and manage user's publisher data in the Google Affiliate Network

google_books.description: View and manage user's books and library in Google Books

google_cloud_storage.description: View and manage user's data stored in Google Cloud Storage

google_drive.description: Full access to all files and folders in the user's Google Drive

google_drive_files.description: Access to files created or opened by the app

google_plus.description: Associate user with its public Google profile

latitude_best.description: View and manage user's best-available current location and location history in Google Latitude

latitude_city.description: View and manage user's city-level current location and location history in Google Latitude

moderator.description: View and manage user's votes, topics, and submissions

offline_access.description: Request a refresh token when the user authorizes your application

orkut.description: View and manage user's friends, applications and profile and status

picasa_web.description: View and manage user's Google photos, videos, photo and video tags and comments

presentations.description: View and manage your Google Slides presentations

profile.description: Name, public profile URL, photo, country, language, and timezone

sites.description: View and manage user's sites on Google Sites

tasks.description: Full access to create, edit, organize, and delete all your tasks

tasks_readonly.description: Read-only access to view your tasks and task lists

url_shortener.description: View, manage and view statistics user's short URLs

webmaster_tools.description: View and manage user's sites and messages, view keywords

youtube_channelmemberships_creator.description: See a list of your current active channel members, their current level, and when they became a member

youtube_forcessl.description: See, edit, and permanently delete your YouTube videos, ratings, comments, and captions

youtubepartner.description: View and manage your assets and associated content on YouTube

youtubepartner_channelaudit.description: View private information of your YouTube channel relevant during the audit process with a YouTube partner

ConnectionOptionsIdpinitiatedSAML.description: Configuration for IdP-Initiated SAML Single Sign-On. When enabled, allows users to initiate login directly from their SAML identity provider without first visiting Auth0. The IdP must include the connection parameter in the post-back URL (Assertion Consumer Service URL).

client_authorizequery.description: The query string sent to the default application

client_id.description: The client ID to use for IdP-initiated login requests.

enabled.description: When true, enables IdP-initiated login support for this SAML connection. Allows users to log in directly from the identity provider without first visiting Auth0.

cheesecake.description: Relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

client_name.description: Relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

domain_aliases.description: Relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

prototocol.description: Relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

signSAMLRequest.description: Relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

test.description: Relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

anyOf.description: Relaxed validation to allow data already in the database
Relaxed validation to allow data already in the database

email.description: Permission to request the user's email address from LINE. When enabled, adds the 'email' scope to OAuth requests. Note: LINE requires special approval to access user email addresses.

profile.description: Permission to request the user's basic profile information from LINE. When enabled, adds the 'profile' scope to OAuth requests. LINE requires this scope to retrieve user display name, profile picture, and status message.

strategy_version.description: The strategy_version property determines which LinkedIn API version and OAuth scopes are used for authentication. Version 1 uses legacy scopes (r_basicprofile, r_fullprofile, r_network), Version 2 uses updated scopes (r_liteprofile, r_basicprofile), and Version 3 uses OpenID Connect scopes (profile, email, openid). If not specified, the connection defaults to Version 3.

anyOf.description: Relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

basic_profile.description: Request the LinkedIn lite profile scope (r_liteprofile) to retrieve member id, localized first/last name, and profile picture. Off by default.

email.description: Request the email address scope (r_emailaddress) to return the member's primary email. Off by default.

full_profile.description: Request the legacy full profile scope (r_fullprofile) for extended attributes. Deprecated by LinkedIn; use only if enabled for your app. Off by default.

network.description: Request legacy network access (first-degree connections). Deprecated by LinkedIn and typically unavailable to new apps. Off by default.

openid.description: Request OpenID Connect authentication support (openid scope). When enabled, the connection will request the 'openid' scope from LinkedIn, allowing the use of OpenID Connect flows for authentication and enabling the issuance of ID tokens. This is off by default and should only be enabled if your LinkedIn application is configured for OpenID Connect.

profile.description: Always-true flag that ensures the LinkedIn profile scope (r_basicprofile/r_liteprofile/profile) is requested.

client_id.description: OAuth 1.0 client identifier issued by the identity provider during application registration. This value identifies your Auth0 connection to the identity provider.

client_secret.description: OAuth 1.0 client secret issued by the identity provider during application registration. Used to authenticate your Auth0 connection when signing requests and exchanging request tokens and verifiers for access tokens. May be null for public clients.

dashboard_icon_url.description: Icon URL for displaying the connection in the Auth0 Dashboard. Must be an HTTPS URL pointing to an image resource used in the management UI.

integration_name.description: Deprecated. Use display_name instead. Previously used for naming custom OAuth2 integrations in the Auth0 Dashboard.

pkce_enabled.description: When true, enables Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) for the authorization code flow. PKCE provides additional security by preventing authorization code interception attacks.

useOauthSpecScope.description: When true, uses space-delimited scopes (per OAuth 2.0 spec) instead of comma-delimited when calling the identity provider's authorization endpoint. Only relevant when using the connection_scope parameter. See https://auth0.com/docs/authenticate/identity-providers/adding-scopes-for-an-external-idp#pass-scopes-to-authorize-endpoint

authorizationURL.description: The URL of the authorization endpoint.

tokenURL.description: The URL of the token endpoint.

ConnectionOptionsOIDCMetadata.description: OpenID Connect Provider Metadata as per https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html#ProviderMetadata

address.description: When enabled, requests the 'address' scope from PayPal to access the user's address information.

email.description: When enabled, requests the 'email' scope from PayPal to access the user's email address.

phone.description: When enabled, requests the 'phone' scope from PayPal to access the user's phone number.

profile.description: When enabled, requests the 'profile' scope from PayPal to access basic profile information including first name, last name, date of birth, time zone, locale, and language. This scope is always enabled by the system.

ConnectionOptionsProtocolEnumTwitter.description: Allowed protocol identifiers for Twitter connections. Use oauth1 for the legacy v1.1 OAuth flow or oauth2 for the newer OAuth 2.0 flow.

disableFieldsMapFix.description: When true, disables the automatic correction of the fieldsMap configuration to ensure proper mapping of SAML attributes to user profile fields. Defaults to false (fieldsMap fix enabled).

disableSignout.description: When true, disables sending SAML logout requests (SingleLogoutService) to the identity provider during user sign-out. The user will be logged out of Auth0 but will remain logged into the identity provider. Defaults to false (federated logout enabled).

storeMultipleCertsFromMetadata.description: When true, multiple signing certificates are extracted from the identity provider's metadata XML and stored in signingCerts. Supports certificate rotation by retaining up to 2 certificates. Defaults to false (only a single certificate is stored).

brute_force_protection.description: Whether brute force protection is enabled

ConnectionOptionsSalesforce.description: Options for the salesforce family of connections (salesforce, salesforce-sandbox, salesforce-community)

profile.description: When enabled, requests the Salesforce profile scope to retrieve basic user information including user_id, organization_id, username, display_name, email, status, photos, and URLs.

anyOf.description: relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

ConnectionOptionsScopeTwitter.description: Array of OAuth 2.0 scopes to request from Twitter during authentication. Use this to request scopes not covered by the predefined scope options.

anyOf.description: relaxed validation to continue to allow existing data in the database

ConnectionOptionsSubjectCommonNameSAML.description: Common Name (CN) from the certificate subject

ConnectionOptionsSubjectOrganizationName.description: Organization Name (O) from the certificate subject

ConnectionOptionsSubjectSAML.description: Certificate Subject Distinguished Name (DN) extracted from the identity provider's signing certificate. Automatically populated with certificate subject fields (CN, O, OU, L, ST, C, etc.) in JSON format during connection creation/update. Used for certificate identification and validation.

offline_access.description: Request long-lived refresh tokens so your app can act on behalf of users even when they’re not actively signed in. Typical Twitter use case: keeping a background service synced without forcing users to reauthorize every session.

profile.description: Pull account profile metadata such as display name, bio, location, and URL so downstream apps can prefill or personalize user experiences.

tweet_read.description: Allow the application to read a user’s public and protected Tweets—required for timelines, analytics, or moderation workflows.

users_read.description: Read non-Tweet user information (e.g., followers/following, account settings) to power relationship graphs or audience insights.

applications.description: When enabled, requests access to user's applications.

applications_create.description: When enabled, requests permission to create applications.

basic.description: When enabled, requests read access to user's basic profile information and contacts list.

birthday.description: When enabled, requests read access to user's birth day, month, and year.

calendars.description: When enabled, requests read access to user's calendars and events.

calendars_update.description: When enabled, requests read and write access to user's calendars and events.

contacts_birthday.description: When enabled, requests read access to contacts' birth day and birth month.

contacts_calendars.description: When enabled, requests read access to user's calendars and shared calendars/events from others.

contacts_create.description: When enabled, requests permission to create new contacts in user's address book.

contacts_photos.description: When enabled, requests read access to user's and shared albums, photos, videos, and audio.

contacts_skydrive.description: When enabled, requests read access to OneDrive files shared by other users.

directory_accessasuser_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to have the same access to information in the directory as the signed-in user.

directory_read_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read data in your organization's directory, such as users, groups, and apps.

directory_readwrite_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read and write data in your organization's directory, such as users and groups.

emails.description: When enabled, requests read access to personal, preferred, and business email addresses.

events_create.description: When enabled, requests permission to create events on user's default calendar.

graph_calendars.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's calendars.

graph_calendars_update.description: When enabled, requests permission to read and write the user's calendars.

graph_contacts.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's contacts.

graph_contacts_update.description: When enabled, requests permission to read and write the user's contacts.

graph_device.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's device information.

graph_device_command.description: When enabled, requests permission to send commands to the user's devices.

graph_emails.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's emails.

graph_emails_update.description: When enabled, requests permission to read and write the user's emails.

graph_files.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's files.

graph_files_all.description: When enabled, requests permission to read all files the user has access to.

graph_files_all_update.description: When enabled, requests permission to read and write all files the user has access to.

graph_files_update.description: When enabled, requests permission to read and write the user's files.

graph_notes.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's OneNote notebooks.

graph_notes_create.description: When enabled, requests permission to create new OneNote notebooks.

graph_notes_update.description: When enabled, requests permission to read and write the user's OneNote notebooks.

graph_tasks.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's tasks.

graph_tasks_update.description: When enabled, requests permission to read and write the user's tasks.

graph_user.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's profile.

graph_user_activity.description: When enabled, requests permission to read the user's activity history.

graph_user_update.description: When enabled, requests permission to read and write the user's profile.

group_read_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read all group properties and memberships.

group_readwrite_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to create groups, read all group properties and memberships, update group properties and memberships, and delete groups.

mail_readwrite_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to create, read, update, and delete all mail in all mailboxes.

mail_send.description: When enabled, allows the app to send mail as users in the organization.

messenger.description: When enabled, requests access to user's Windows Live Messenger data.

offline_access.description: When enabled, requests a refresh token for offline access.

phone_numbers.description: When enabled, requests read access to personal, business, and mobile phone numbers.

photos.description: When enabled, requests read access to user's photos, videos, audio, and albums.

postal_addresses.description: When enabled, requests read access to personal and business postal addresses.

rolemanagement_read_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read the role-based access control (RBAC) settings for your company's directory.

rolemanagement_readwrite_directory.description: When enabled, allows the app to read and write the role-based access control (RBAC) settings for your company's directory.

share.description: When enabled, requests permission to share content with other users.

signin.description: When enabled, provides single sign-in behavior for users already signed into their Microsoft account.

sites_read_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read documents and list items in all SharePoint site collections.

sites_readwrite_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to create, read, update, and delete documents and list items in all SharePoint site collections.

skydrive.description: When enabled, requests read access to user's files stored on OneDrive.

skydrive_update.description: When enabled, requests read and write access to user's OneDrive files.

team_readbasic_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read the names and descriptions of all teams.

team_readwrite_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read and write all teams' information and change team membership.

user_read_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read the full set of profile properties, reports, and managers of all users.

user_readbasic_all.description: When enabled, allows the app to read a basic set of profile properties of all users in the directory.

work_profile.description: When enabled, requests read access to employer and work position information.

Servers SHOULD support none and RS256.

ConnectionSendBackChannelNonce.description: When true and type is 'back_channel', includes a cryptographic nonce in authorization requests to prevent replay attacks. The identity provider must include this nonce in the ID token for validation.

allOf.description: URL of a page containing human-readable information that developers might want or need to know when using the OpenID Provider. In particular, if the OpenID Provider does not support Dynamic Client Registration, then information on how to register Clients needs to be provided in this documentation.

ConnectionSetUserRootAttributesEnum.description: When using an external IdP, this flag determines  whether 'name', 'given_name', 'family_name', 'nickname', and 'picture' attributes are updated. In addition, it also determines whether the user is created when user doesnt exist previously. Possible values are 'on_each_login' (default value, it configures the connection to automatically create the user if necessary and update the root attributes from the external IdP with each user login. When this setting is used, root attributes cannot be independently updated), 'on_first_login' (configures the connection to create the user and set the root attributes on first login only, allowing them to be independently updated thereafter), and 'never_on_login' (configures the connection not to create the user and not to set the root attributes from the external IdP, allowing them to be independently updated).

Fully defined credentials that will be enabled on the client for mTLS authentication utilizing self-signed certificate.

This field allows you to enter other origins if necessary. You can also use wildcards at the subdomain level (e.g., https://*.contoso.com). skip_non_verifiable_callback_uri_confirm.description:
Controls whether a confirmation prompt is shown during login flows when the redirect URI uses non-verifiable callback URIs (for example, a custom URI schema such as `myapp://`, or `localhost`).
If set to true, a confirmation prompt will not be shown. We recommend that this is set to false for improved protection from malicious apps.
See https://auth0.com/docs/secure/security-guidance/measures-against-app-impersonation for more information.

GetAvailableIntegrationResponseContent.description:
Integration defines a self contained functioning unit which partners
publish. A partner may create one or many of these integrations.

id.description:
id is a system generated GUID. This same ID is designed to be federated in
all the applicable localities.

catalog_id.description: catalog_id refers to the ID in the marketplace catalog

url_slug.description: url_slug refers to the url_slug in the marketplace catalog

partner_id.description:
partner_id is the foreign key reference to the partner account this
integration belongs to.

name.description:
name is the integration name, which will be used for display purposes in
the marketplace.

To start we're going to make sure the display name is at least 3
characters. Can adjust this easily later.

description.description:
description adds more text for the integration name -- also relevant for
the marketplace listing.

short_description.description: short_description is the brief description of the integration, which is used for display purposes in cards

tenant.description: The name of the tenant

disabled.description: Whether the provider is enabled (false) or disabled (true).

created_at.description: The provider's creation date and time in ISO 8601 format

updated_at.description: The date and time of the last update to the provider in ISO 8601 format

favicon_url.description: URL for the favicon. Must use HTTPS.

logo_url.description: URL for the logo. Must use HTTPS.

custom_css_url.description: URL for the custom CSS file. Must use HTTPS.

enabled.description: Whether or not breached password detection is active.

shields.description: Action to take when a breached password is detected during a login. Possible values: block, user_notification, admin_notification.

admin_notification_frequency.description: When "admin_notification" is enabled, determines how often email notifications are sent. Possible values: immediately, daily, weekly, monthly.

enabled.description: Whether or not brute force attack protections are active.

shields.description: Action to take when a brute force protection threshold is violated. Possible values: block, user_notification.

allowlist.description: List of trusted IP addresses that will not have attack protection enforced against them.

expires_at.description: The ISO 8601 formatted date representing the expiration of the credential.

client_id.description: ID of the client.

scope.description: Scopes allowed for this client grant.

allow_any_organization.description: If enabled, any organization can be used with this grant. If disabled (default), the grant must be explicitly assigned to the desired organizations.

default_for.description: Specifies that the client grant is the default for the target audience (API identifier) for the specified group of clients. The only permissible value is `third_party_clients`.

is_system.description: If enabled, this grant is a special grant created by Auth0. It cannot be modified or deleted directly.

allow_all_scopes.description: If enabled, all scopes configured on the resource server are allowed for this grant.

callbacks.description: Comma-separated list of URLs whitelisted for Auth0 to use as a callback to the client after authentication.

allowed_origins.description: Comma-separated list of URLs allowed to make requests from JavaScript to Auth0 API (typically used with CORS). By default, all your callback URLs will be allowed. This field allows you to enter other origins if necessary. You can also use wildcards at the subdomain level (e.g., https://*.contoso.com). If set to true, a confirmation prompt will not be shown. We recommend that this is set to false for improved protection from malicious apps.

GetInstalledIntegrationResponseContent.description:
InstalledIntegration is the materialization of an Integration being
installed for a specified tenant account.  This materialization can only be
done once per tenant (e.g. they can only have the integration installed once
at any given time).

Integrations can be re-installed, which re-materializes a different entity
if they do so. The act of uninstalling means deleting this entity, and the
act of installing means creating this entity.

TenantTriggers are also updated when upgrades happen assuming any of the
action versions of the integration were updated. This means that as soon as
installed integrations have been upgraded automatically, they would start
using the latest action versions published by the partner in their
integration release.

id.description:
id is a GUID attached to the lifetime of an installed integration.
Uninstalling an integration would delete that specific id.

integration_id.description: integration_id is the fk reference to the Integration entity.

integration_release_id.description: integration_release_id is the fk reference to the IntegrationRelease entity.

major_version.description:
major_version is the major version of the integration that is being installed. At
the time of installation, the artifacts (actions) from the latest release of
that major version will be installed into a tenant's library.

configuration.description: The configuration values required by the integration.

client_id.description: ID of this client.

name.description: Name of this client (min length: 1 character, does not allow `<` or `>`).

callbacks.description: Comma-separated list of URLs whitelisted for Auth0 to use as a callback to the client after authentication.

allowed_logout_urls.description: Comma-separated list of URLs that are valid to redirect to after logout from Auth0. Wildcards are allowed for subdomains.

allowed_origins.description: Comma-separated list of URLs allowed to make requests from JavaScript to Auth0 API (typically used with CORS). By default, all your callback URLs will be allowed. This field allows you to enter other origins if necessary. You can also use wildcards at the subdomain level (e.g., https://*.contoso.com). Query strings and hash information are not taken into account when validating these URLs.

web_origins.description: Comma-separated list of allowed origins for use with Cross-Origin Authentication, Device Flow, and web message response mode.

Controls whether a confirmation prompt is shown during login flows when the redirect URI uses non-verifiable callback URIs (for example, a custom URI schema such as `myapp://`, or `localhost`).

InstallIntegrationResponseContent.description:
InstalledIntegration is the materialization of an Integration being
installed for a specified tenant account.  This materialization can only be
done once per tenant (e.g. they can only have the integration installed once
at any given time).

Integrations can be re-installed, which re-materializes a different entity
if they do so. The act of uninstalling means deleting this entity, and the
act of installing means creating this entity.

TenantTriggers are also updated when upgrades happen assuming any of the
action versions of the integration were updated. This means that as soon as
installed integrations have been upgraded automatically, they would start
using the latest action versions published by the partner in their
integration release.

skip_non_verifiable_callback_uri_confirm.description:
Controls whether a confirmation prompt is shown during login flows when the redirect URI uses non-verifiable callback URIs (for example, a custom URI schema such as `myapp://`, or `localhost`).
If set to true, a confirmation prompt will not be shown. We recommend that this is set to false for improved protection from malicious apps.
See https://auth0.com/docs/secure/security-guidance/measures-against-app-impersonation for more information.

Controls whether a confirmation prompt is shown during login flows when the redirect URI uses non-verifiable callback URIs (for example, a custom URI schema such as `myapp://`, or `localhost`).
If set to true, a confirmation prompt will not be shown. We recommend that this is set to false for improved protection from malicious apps.
